name: claude-context (Zilliz)
description: MCP server that indexes a repo into Milvus/Zilliz for embedding-based retrieval

developer-org.comment: Built by Zilliz Tech, the company behind the Milvus vector database.

implementation-language.comment: Core and MCP server are TypeScript (~72% of repo); a smaller Python share exists but the installable/runnable core is TypeScript (npm @zilliz/claude-context-mcp / @zilliz/claude-context-core).

first-release.comment: Repository created / project announced June 2025; no tagged GitHub releases exist, so this is the public-launch month.

website.comment: GitHub repo serves as the primary landing/product page; no separate marketing site.

github-stars.comment: ~12.1k stars captured 2026-07-09; one of the most-starred tools in this category.

form-factor.comment: Ships as an MCP server (@zilliz/claude-context-mcp), a VS Code extension, and a reusable core library (@zilliz/claude-context-core).

standalone-usable.comment: Any MCP-capable agent can add it; not welded to a single host.

bundled-host.comment: Standalone tool, not a bundled engine.

install-mechanism.comment: npm for the MCP server/core; VS Code Marketplace for the editor extension. Milvus backend can be run via Docker separately.

retrieval-technique.comment: Hybrid retrieval fuses BM25 sparse (lexical) with dense-vector (embeddings) search in one path.

query-interface.comment: Agents call an MCP search tool with a natural-language query.

returns.comment: Returns relevant code snippets/chunks rather than whole files.

structural-awareness.comment: Uses tree-sitter / AST parsing to chunk around functions, classes and methods.

chunking-strategy.comment: AST-based chunking around semantic units; falls back to LangChain RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter when AST parsing fails.

reranking.comment: Uses hybrid (BM25 + dense) fusion but no documented separate second-stage cross-encoder/LLM reranker.

index-location.comment: Index lives in a self-hosted local Milvus or in managed Zilliz Cloud.

incremental-updates.comment: Merkle-tree change detection re-indexes only changed files.

real-time-watch.comment: Re-index/sync is triggered (Merkle-based), not a continuous file watcher, per docs as of 2026-07-09.

vector-store.comment: Milvus (open-source, self-hosted) or Zilliz Cloud (managed Milvus).

embedding-model.value: Configurable: OpenAI (default, e.g. text-embedding-3), VoyageAI (code-oriented), Gemini, or Ollama (local)

mcp-support.comment: Ships a full MCP server implementation.

agent-compatibility.comment: Primarily targets Claude Code; documented to work with Cursor, Cline, Windsurf and other MCP clients (15+).

ide-integrations.comment: Official VS Code extension; otherwise surfaces through MCP-capable agents.

language-agnostic.comment: AST chunking depends on tree-sitter parser support; falls back to a generic text splitter for unsupported languages, so quality is language-dependent.

supported-languages.value: tree-sitter: TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Java, C++, C#, Go, Rust, PHP, Ruby, Swift, Kotlin, Scala, Markdown; generic text-splitter fallback otherwise

code-leaves-machine.comment: In the default setup code chunks are sent to a hosted embedding API (OpenAI) and optionally to Zilliz Cloud; only embeddings/snippets leave, not necessarily the whole repo. A fully local Ollama + self-hosted Milvus path avoids any egress.

local-only-option.comment: Ollama local embeddings + self-hosted Milvus enable a fully offline configuration.

self-hostable.comment: OSS core plus self-hosted Milvus can run entirely on the user's own infrastructure.

telemetry.comment: No telemetry posture documented in the repo as of 2026-07-09.

max-repo-scale.comment: Positioned for large/whole codebases; Milvus/Zilliz Cloud backing supports scaling, though no explicit monorepo-scale benchmark is published.

pricing-model.comment: Tool is free OSS; you supply/pay for the embedding API and optionally a Zilliz Cloud plan (free serverless tier available).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Core is free/OSS; costs are external embedding API usage and optional Zilliz Cloud (which has a free tier).

maturity.comment: Popular and active (~12k stars) but ~1 year old, rapidly evolving, and without tagged stable releases.

active-maintenance.comment: Frequent commits and open PRs within the last 90 days as of 2026-07-09.

community-size.comment: ~12k stars, 800+ forks; among the most-adopted tools in this category.
